userStories.title: Config field + threshold check + warning log
userStories.description: Add `quality.scopeTestThreshold` to QualityConfigSchema with default 10. In ScopedStrategy.verify(), after getChangedSourceFiles(), check source file count against threshold. When exceeded, log a warning with file count and threshold, then execute quality.commands.test (full suite) instead of building scoped command.
acceptanceCriteria: ScopedStrategy.verify() with 3 source files and threshold 10 proceeds to scoped test mapping (no fallback)
ScopedStrategy.verify() with 12 source files and threshold 10 executes quality.commands.test instead of scoped command
When fallback triggers, logger.warn("verify[scoped]", ...) is called with message containing file count and threshold
When fallback triggers, the test command used is quality.commands.test (not the scoped file list)

userStories.title: Story metric scopeTestFallback flag
userStories.description: When ScopedStrategy.verify() falls back to full suite due to threshold, set scopeTestFallback: true on the verify result metadata so it propagates to StoryMetrics. Add scopeTestFallback?: boolean to VerifyResult and StoryMetrics interfaces, and propagate in collectStoryMetrics().
acceptanceCriteria: StoryMetrics interface includes optional scopeTestFallback?: boolean
VerifyResult interface includes optional scopeTestFallback?: boolean
When scoped strategy falls back due to threshold, the returned verify result includes scopeTestFallback: true in metadata
collectStoryMetrics() propagates scopeTestFallback from verify result to StoryMetrics
When scoped strategy runs normally (no fallback), scopeTestFallback is absent from StoryMetrics

analysis:
The feature requires modifying 4 files across the verification and metrics subsystems.

**Key files to modify:**

1. **`src/config/schemas.ts`** (line ~144): Add `scopeTestThreshold: z.number().int().min(1).default(10)` to `QualityConfigSchema`. This is a simple additive field with a sensible default of 10.

2. **`src/verification/strategies/scoped.ts`** (lines 54-144): Add threshold check in `ScopedStrategy.execute()` after `getChangedSourceFiles()` returns (line 69). The check must:
   - Get threshold from `ctx.config?.quality?.scopeTestThreshold ?? 10`
   - When exceeded, set `effectiveCommand = ctx.testCommand` and `isFullSuite = true`
   - Log warning via `logger.warn("verify[scoped]", ...)` with fileCount and threshold
   - Set a flag `_thresholdExceeded = true` to bypass the subsequent "No mapped tests" log and deferred-mode early-return that would incorrectly skip execution
   - The threshold check must be placed BEFORE the pass1/pass2 mapping logic so we skip mapping entirely when threshold is exceeded

3. **`src/verification/orchestrator-types.ts`** (line ~62): Add `scopeTestFallback?: boolean` to `VerifyResult` interface so the flag can propagate from ScopedStrategy to metrics collection.

4. **`src/metrics/types.ts`** (line ~10): Add `scopeTestFallback?: boolean` to `StoryMetrics` interface.

5. **`src/metrics/tracker.ts`** (line ~81): In `collectStoryMetrics()`, propagate `scopeTestFallback` from `ctx.verifyResult?.scopeTestFallback` to the returned `StoryMetrics` object.

**Architecture decisions:**

- The `_thresholdExceeded` flag in ScopedStrategy is necessary because `isFullSuite = true` is reused for both "no tests mapped" and "threshold exceeded" cases, but they need different handling:
  - "No tests mapped" + deferred mode â†’ skip (defer to run-end)
  - "Threshold exceeded" â†’ always run full suite immediately (don't defer)
  - "No tests mapped" + per-story mode â†’ run full suite
- Without `_thresholdExceeded`, the deferred-mode check at line 92 (`isFullSuite && regressionMode === "deferred"`) would incorrectly skip execution when threshold is exceeded and mode is deferred.

**Dependency order:**
- US-001 must be implemented before US-002 since US-002 depends on the field existing in the schema to read from.

**Testing approach:**
- The `_scopedDeps` object in scoped.ts provides injectable dependencies for testing (getChangedSourceFiles, regression, etc.)
- Tests can mock `_scopedDeps.getChangedSourceFiles` to return arrays of specific lengths
- Tests can capture logger calls to verify warning is logged
- Tests can inspect the result object for `scopeTestFallback` flag
